title: Rounding up the space unicorns
summary: Billion-dollar space startups, once rare, are now multiplying across new orbital markets. This trend highlights the growing commercialization and investment in the space industry.

title: NASA interested in Hubble reboost if costs can be reduced
summary: NASA is considering a reboost mission for the Hubble Space Telescope to extend its operational life, provided the costs can be significantly reduced. The agency is already preparing a similar attempt for another astronomy spacecraft in a decaying orbit.

title: NASA concerns about Russian repairs prompted ISS safe haven decision
summary: NASA instructed ISS astronauts to take shelter in a Crew Dragon spacecraft due to concerns over Russian cosmonauts' repairs, which were deemed to pose an 'elevated risk' to the station.

title: Leaf Space partners with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test connectivity service
summary: Leaf Space has partnered with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test a new space connectivity service called TreeNet. The service aims to improve seamless space communications.

title: Astronauts briefly shelter in Dragon during ISS leak repair
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station were instructed to briefly shelter in a Dragon spacecraft while cosmonauts repaired an air leak in a Russian module on June 5.

title: Next Ariane 6 launch to carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites using upgraded boosters
summary: Amazon will launch 36 of its Leo broadband satellites on the next Ariane 6 mission, marking the largest payload to date for the service. The flight will also debut upgraded boosters for the Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Let’s build the moon base, but not lose sight of Mars
summary: NASA and the space industry are experiencing significant advancements, with the Artemis 2 crew completing a historic lunar flyby. The focus remains on building a moon base while maintaining Mars as a long-term goal.

title: Qianfan constellation deployment hits 200 satellites with Long March 8 and 6A launches
summary: The Qianfan constellation, led by Shanghai, reached 200 satellites with two recent Long March launches (8 and 6A). This follows two experimental flights and marks a significant milestone in its deployment.

title: Satellite maker Apex’s valuation rises to $2.3 billion after latest $200 million raise
summary: Satellite manufacturer Apex raised $200 million in a funding round, increasing its valuation to $2.3 billion. The company plans to expand its in-house satellite production capabilities with the new capital.

title: The Exploration Company completes drop test of Nyx capsule
summary: The Exploration Company successfully conducted a drop test of its Nyx spacecraft, designed for cargo transport to low Earth orbit. The test is a precursor to a planned 2028 test flight.

title: 10 best Spanish beaches to see the total solar eclipse 2026
summary: On August 12, 2026, several Spanish beaches will experience totality during a solar eclipse. This event is expected to attract significant attention from astronomers and tourists.

title: Could meteor storms harm NASA's future moon missions?
summary: NASA may delay future moon missions if a major meteor storm is forecasted during crew activities. Crews would remain inside their spacecraft or habitats until the storm passes.

title: Who is Lobo, the cigar-smoking alien bounty hunter in 'Supergirl'?
summary: Jason Momoa will star as Lobo, a cigar-smoking alien bounty hunter, in the upcoming 'Supergirl' series. Lobo is a well-known DC Comics character known for his ruthless and chaotic nature.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 213 — Live From ISDC With Gerry Griffin
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' podcast features Apollo legend Gerry Griffin discussing space topics live from the International Space Development Conference (ISDC) in McLean, Virginia.

title: Neil deGrasse Tyson narrates new trailer for upcoming Broadway musical, 'Galileo'
summary: Neil deGrasse Tyson narrates a new trailer for the upcoming Broadway musical 'Galileo', featuring Raúl Esparza in the lead role as the Renaissance astronomer. The show is set to premiere in the fall.

title: I resisted smart telescopes for years — then one changed my life
summary: The article discusses the author's positive experience with smart telescopes, highlighting their potential to enhance stargazing during the spring season. It emphasizes the appeal of dark skies for deep-sky observation.

title: Going supersonic! NASA's X-59 jet breaks sound barrier for the 1st time
summary: NASA's X-59 jet achieved supersonic flight for the first time on June 5, reaching a speed of Mach 1.1. This milestone marks a significant step in quiet supersonic technology development.

title: DC Studios lights the fuse on a sensational final 'Supergirl' trailer and it's totally peak (video)
summary: DC Studios released the final trailer for the upcoming 'Supergirl' project, highlighting a key dialogue about heroism. The trailer is generating significant buzz ahead of its release.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions despite the loss of its New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This marks a key milestone in advancing the spacecraft's reusability capabilities.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: The Space Force is assessing damage and potential future risks related to an anomaly. Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) has cleared the Eastern Range for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are scheduled, with SpaceX and Chinese entities leading the launches.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances our understanding of early cosmic phenomena.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a major anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident occurred during a planned hot-fire test of the rocket.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, involving rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for follow-up missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news source is TEC Business to Business website, but no specific space industry news is provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: The 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway marks the latest success of ESA's Prodex programme, which supports scientific research institutes in European space missions. Prodex is a European Space Agency initiative enabling high-skilled research in space science.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring materials can withstand space conditions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section plays a key role in spacecraft assembly.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' is vague and lacks context. It may refer to a technological advancement or a structural feature in space industry components.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry article discusses a critical parachute test for a Mars mission, involving a 'bake-out' to ensure the parachute's functionality in extreme conditions. The test aims to validate the parachute's deployment system ahead of an upcoming launch.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is sterilizing a 35-meter diameter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling improved docking and debris removal. This innovation could significantly enhance satellite servicing and space debris mitigation efforts.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate rendezvous capabilities with an asteroid. This mission will pave the way for future asteroid mining efforts before 2030.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex has raised $200 million in a funding round, valuing the company at $2.3 billion. This is the third time the company has raised $200 million in approximately 14 months.

title: US Commercial Space Stations Expand to Europe
summary: The article discusses the expansion of US commercial space stations into Europe, highlighting Europe's continued access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) despite the International Space Station (ISS) nearing the end of its operational life.

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 performance was discussed by CFO and President Ashley Johnson, highlighting their differentiation in revenue recognition compared to hardware-focused companies. The interview emphasized Planet's unique approach to monetizing satellite data rather than relying solely on hardware sales.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company completed a Nyx drop test on May 19 to evaluate parachute performance and vehicle dynamics during a controlled Earth landing. The test used a dedicated drop-test vehicle to simulate the capsule's descent.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a scheduled July launch.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times larger than its predecessor Condor-XL. This new bus is designed to cater to the emerging orbital-data-center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to return to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement strategy. The move follows industry feedback influencing the agency's approach.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to resume flight operations by the end of the year after assessing damage to its launch infrastructure. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the return-to-flight timeline in a post on X.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating two Pentagon offices, the Space Development Agency (SDA) and the Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO), which are focused on accelerating space acquisition. This move could significantly impact the Pentagon's space programs and acquisition strategies.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo 50% Deployment Waiver, With Conditions on Spectrum Priority
summary: The FCC granted Amazon a waiver to deploy 50% of its Project Kuiper satellite constellation by the end of July, but with conditions on spectrum priority. This decision impacts Amazon's satellite broadband initiative.

title: Apex Hits $2.3B Valuation With Latest Funding Raise
summary: Apex Space, a satellite bus manufacturer, raised an additional $200 million in capital, bringing its valuation to $2.3 billion. This funding round more than doubles the company's previous valuation.

title: Aalyria to Support Orchestration for NASA SCaN Program
summary: NASA selected Aalyria to support orchestration for its Space Communications and Navigation (SCaN) Program, beginning with the PExT demonstration mission. The PExT mission aims to advance polylingual experimental terminal capabilities.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite services for maritime applications. The collaboration aims to enhance connectivity solutions in the maritime sector.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million option for maritime domain awareness analytics. This follows an existing contract for AI-enabled data analytics for U.S. Combatant Commands.

title: Infinite Orbits and Open Cosmos Plan ‘Tom & Jerry’ Space Rendezvous Mission
summary: Infinite Orbits has selected Open Cosmos to develop two satellites, 'Tom & Jerry,' for a maneuvering and inspection mission in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO). The mission aims to demonstrate space rendezvous capabilities.

title: Mbryonics and NTT to Collaborate on Optical Communications Infrastructure
summary: Mbryonics, an Irish photonics company, will collaborate with NTT to develop a high-speed optical communications infrastructure for space. The partnership is formalized under a new Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) announced on Thursday.

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a new high-power satellite platform named 'Ultra' aimed at supporting orbital data centers. The first mission for this platform is scheduled for 2028.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of satellite propulsion systems.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense successfully launched an IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, raising $650 million by offering 32.5 million shares. The company is an aerospace and defense firm that began trading publicly.
